title: Autorepression r vs TE multiplier
caption: Pooled Pearson correlation (DnaA-TF promoter binding ↔ dnaA mRNA copy number) across the second half of each run. Green pass band: r ≤ -0.3 (negative correlation means binding suppresses mRNA — autorepression).
simulations: Same TE-multiplier sweep as chart 01. For each run, the time series of DnaA-TF promoter binding (listener: tf_binding.bound_DnaA_at_dnaA_promoter) and dnaA mRNA copy number (listener: rna_counts.mRNA_counts[EG10235_RNA]) are extracted, restricted to the second half (cell-state-stabilised window), and Pearson-correlated. Per-seed r values are then pooled to a single multiplier-level statistic.
interpretation: A healthy autorepression loop produces negative r (high binding → low mRNA next tick). At low TE (1×-20×) the correlation is strongly negative and the loop is functional. Between 20× and 25× the sign flips and r climbs toward zero — DnaA has saturated its own promoter boxes and the cell can no longer use binding to throttle transcription. Combined with chart 01, this is why a single TE knob can't simultaneously satisfy the count gate (needs ≥50×) and the autorepression gate (needs <25×).
